Module com.example.miniproyectosudoku6x6
Package com.example.miniproyectosudoku6x6.model

Class GeneradorSudoku

java.lang.Object
com.example.miniproyectosudoku6x6.model.GeneradorSudoku
public class GeneradorSudoku extends Object
Generates random valid 6x6 Sudoku boards.

The generation algorithm is based on classic backtracking: the generator iterates over every cell of an empty board and tries to place numbers from 1 to 6 in a randomized order. If a number breaks any Sudoku rule at the current position, the generator backtracks to the previous cell and tries a different value. Once every cell holds a valid number, the board is fully solved.

After producing a solved board, the generator selects exactly two random cells per 2x3 block as the initial clues that the player will see when the game starts. Those cells are marked as fixed (non-editable) and the remaining cells are cleared so the player can fill them in.

Because the order in which numbers are tried is randomized, every call to generarNuevoTablero() produces a different starting configuration, satisfying user story HU-1.

This class belongs to the model layer and remains completely independent of any UI framework.

    • PISTAS_POR_BLOQUE

      public static final int PISTAS_POR_BLOQUE
      Number of fixed clues placed per 2x3 block.
      See Also:
      • Constant Field Values

    • aleatorio

      private final Random aleatorio
      Random number generator used throughout the generation process.

  • Constructor Details

    • GeneradorSudoku

      public GeneradorSudoku()
      Constructs a new generator with a fresh random source.

  • Method Details

    • generarNuevoTablero

      public TableroSudoku generarNuevoTablero()
      Generates a brand-new Sudoku board ready to be played.

      The returned board has exactly 2 fixed clues per 2x3 block (12 in total), and the remaining cells are empty and editable by the player.

      Returns:
      a new playable Sudoku board

    • generarTableroResuelto

      public TableroSudoku generarTableroResuelto()
      Generates a fully solved Sudoku board with no empty cells and no fixed clues. This is useful for hint generation in later phases.
      Returns:
      a new fully solved Sudoku board

    • llenarTableroRecursivo

      private boolean llenarTableroRecursivo(TableroSudoku tablero, int fila, int columna)
      Recursive backtracking method that fills the board with a valid configuration. Numbers from 1 to 6 are tried in a random order at each cell to guarantee that successive calls produce different boards.
      Parameters:
      tablero - the board being filled
      fila - the row of the current cell being processed
      columna - the column of the current cell being processed
      Returns:
      true if the board could be filled from this point onwards, false otherwise (which triggers backtracking)

    • seleccionarPistasIniciales

      private void seleccionarPistasIniciales(TableroSudoku tablero)
      Selects exactly PISTAS_POR_BLOQUE random cells per 2x3 block and marks them as fixed clues. All remaining cells are cleared so that the player can fill them in.
      Parameters:
      tablero - the board to prepare for play

    • generarOrdenAleatorio

      private List<Integer> generarOrdenAleatorio()
      Produces a list with the numbers from 1 to 6 in a random order. Used by the backtracking algorithm to ensure each generated board is different from the previous ones.
      Returns:
      a shuffled list containing the integers from 1 to 6
